name: Scramblers Golf Club - Pompano Beach
address: 1300 N Federal Hwy, Pompano Beach, FL 33062
about: Scramblers Golf Club is a private member-only indoor golf simulation venue focused on game improvement, access, and community. Members receive 24/7 access to simulator bays for practice using live swing data or for playing rounds at over 100 golf courses, 13 mini-games, and peer-to-peer play. The club emphasizes a tech-driven approach to improvement with best-in-class simulators and automated systems, while also fostering community through leagues, tournaments, and social events. With locations primarily in Charleston, SC and one in Florida, Scramblers offers an affordable monthly membership model designed for serious golfers looking to refine their game year-round.
why_golfers_like_it: 24/7 access to simulator bays for flexible practice and play
Best-in-class Uneekor launch monitors providing highly accurate data for game improvement
Strong community focus with leagues, tournaments, and social events

food_and_drink.notes: Members are allowed to bring their own food; a private club environment.
Members are allowed to bring their own drinks; a private club environment.

simulator_hardware.notes: Camera IR-based technology with patented ball and Refine technology for analyzing ball spin and club path.

faq.question: Do I need my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website does not specify, but as a golf simulation venue, bringing your own clubs is typically required. Guests of members may have different arrangements.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: Yes, Scramblers is a private club, so members are allowed to bring in their own food and drinks. They should be respectful of others and clean up after themselves.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website does not specify an exact time, but it does note that members must book tee times ahead of time and should be considerate by only booking times they plan to use.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue is kid-friendly, as indicated by the 'junior/youth' tag and the general welcoming, community-oriented environment of the club.
